input.command: coop-send agent2 "agent1: ack. My change to first()/last() only adds an Option<Str> #[named] default param and does val.or(default) before ok_or_else. Since we're likely in separate sandboxes producing independent patch.txt, no action needed unless you're told to merge. Let me know if that changes."

content.text: Now let's finalize by writing the diff.

content.content:
diff --git a/crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs b/crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs
index d90b6f206..c11c9329d 100644
--- a/crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs
+++ b/crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs
@@ -179,22 +179,34 @@ impl Str {
     /// Extracts the first grapheme cluster of the string.
     /// Fails with an error if the string is empty.
     #[func]
-    pub fn first(&self) -> StrResult<Str> {
+    pub fn first(
+        &self,
+        /// A default value to return if the string is empty.
+        #[named]
+        default: Option<Str>,
+    ) -> StrResult<Str> {
         self.0
             .graphemes(true)
             .next()
             .map(Into::into)
+            .or(default)
             .ok_or_else(string_is_empty)
     }
 
     /// Extracts the last grapheme cluster of the string.
     /// Fails with an error if the string is empty.
     #[func]
-    pub fn last(&self) -> StrResult<Str> {
+    pub fn last(
+        &self,
+        /// A default value to return if the string is empty.
+        #[named]
+        default: Option<Str>,
+    ) -> StrResult<Str> {
         self.0
             .graphemes(true)
             .next_back()
             .map(Into::into)
+            .or(default)
             .ok_or_else(string_is_empty)
     }

content.text: Diff written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`. Implemented the `default` argument for `str.first()` and `str.last()` in `crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs`, following the existing `#[named] default: Option<T>` convention used by `str.at()`. No Rust toolchain was available in this sandbox to run `cargo check`, but the change is minimal and mirrors an established pattern in the same file. Flagged the overlap with agent2 (who's touching the same two methods for a `strip` param) and confirmed we appear to be working in separate sandboxes with independently submitted patches.
